title: Implement SOGRAND soft-output computation
description:
Implement the SOGRAND (Soft-Output GRAND) layer on top of ORBGRAND, providing per-block APP and per-bit LLR outputs.

## Per-Block APP Computation

Per Theorem 1 / Corollary 1, for each codeword found at query position `q_i` with noise pattern `z^{n,q_i}`:

- Compute noise probability `p(z^{n,q_i} | r^n)`
- Track cumulative noise probability: `sum_{j=1}^{q_L} p(z^{n,j} | r^n)`
- APP for i-th list element: `P(correct=i) = p_i / (sum_list + (1 - sum_cumulative) * (2^k - 1)/(2^n - 1))`
- Probability correct codeword is NOT in list: `P(C\L | r^n) = ((1 - sum_cumulative) * (2^k-1)/(2^n-1)) / denominator`

## Per-Bit APP LLR Output

Per eq. 17:

```
L'_{APP,i} = log(
  (sum of P(c) where c_i=0 in list + P(C\L)*p(X_i=0|r_i)) /
  (sum of P(c) where c_i=1 in list + P(C\L)*p(X_i=1|r_i))
)
```

The "not found" term `P(C\L)` is the key innovation — it allows accurate soft-output even with list size L=1.

## SISO Interface

Takes `(L_Ch + L_A)` as input LLRs, returns `L_APP` and extrinsic `L_E = L_APP - L_A - L_Ch`. Required by the product code turbo decoder.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Soft-output LLRs are computed correctly according to eq. 17.
- The "not found" probability term is correctly included.
- Can reproduce Fig 2 from the paper (predicted vs empirical list-BLER) to validate the APP formula.
- SISO interface correctly computes extrinsic information.
- Works with list sizes L=1, 2, 4.
- Numerical stability: handle log-domain computation to avoid underflow with large n.

## Location

Extend the `grand` module.
